extDesc.message: Scans and cleans suspicious website notification permissions. Protects against spam and scam ads in real-time.

togglePromptLabel.message: Allow sites to ask for notifications

realtimeGuardDesc.message: Instantly blocks notification access if a non-whitelisted site gains permission.

silentGuardianDesc.message: Quietly blocks notifications from non-whitelisted sites in the background without showing any alerts.

scanningMessage.message: Scanning browsing history, bookmarks, and open tabs...

emptyStateMsg.message: Click Scan to check if any unverified websites have permission to send notifications.

safeStateMsg.message:
No unverified notification permissions detected.

If you were seeing suspicious warnings or unwanted advertisements, they may have been spam messages exploiting browser notification settings.

Once the source domains are blocked, those notifications will stop appearing.

confirmClearAllRules.message: This will clear all site-specific notification blocks and settings created by NotiSpam Cleaner, reverting them to browser defaults. Are you sure?

confirmResetAll.message: Are you sure you want to revoke notification permissions for all unverified websites?

confirmResetActiveDomain.message: This website is currently open in your browser. Resetting its notification settings in this state may cause the automatic block warning to reappear immediately. Please close the website's tab first, then perform the reset operation. Do you want to continue?

guardNotificationMessage.message: Automatically blocked notification permissions for unverified site "$domain$". Click below to trust this site if it was a mistake.

tooltipAllowedBrowser.message: This permission is stored in the browser. It will disappear from this scan if the site is closed or cleared from history, but you can always manage it in browser settings.

emptyStateBlockedMsg.message: No websites are currently blocked by NotiSpam Cleaner.

helpDesc.message:
Malicious sites often trick you into clicking "Allow" by showing fake Captchas ("Click Allow to verify you are not a robot") or fake security alerts. Once permitted, they push malicious popups and fake antivirus notifications directly to your screen as website notifications. NotiSpam Cleaner easily flags these, lets you clean them with one click, and guards you dynamically.

*Note: Settings in NotiSpam Cleaner (Block/Trust) sync directly with your browser's native notification settings database. Changes made here are immediately applied to the browser, and vice versa.

settingsLinkNotice.message: Due to browser limitations, allowed sites that are no longer in your active tabs or recent history cannot be scanned (including reset sites). To manage the complete list, please open browser settings.
